channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: AI Could Save Construction $1 Trillion a Year—Here’s How
item.description: More than $1 trillion—around 10 percent of annual construction spend—is wasted each year because of mistakes that require redoing the work.

item.title: What Mick Cronin’s Moment Teaches About Leadership Under Pressure
item.description: When passion stops reinforcing your message and starts distracting from it, it becomes noise.

item.title: GDP growth sharply slows to 1.4%, less than half of fourth quarter expectations
item.description: Economists say the small bump in unemployment rates is due to the Trump Administration's immigration crackdown stalling population growth.

item.title: Trump’s global tariffs were just struck down by the Supreme Court. Here’s what that means
item.description: The Supreme Court has struck down President Donald Trump’s far-reaching global tariffs , handing him a significant loss on an issue crucial to his eco...

item.title: How technology is reinventing global payments
item.description: In a rapidly evolving financial landscape, technology is transforming how money moves—making payments of tomorrow faster, smarter, and easier than eve...

item.title: A CEO Has 99 Problems. An HR Leader Shouldn’t Be One
item.description: CEO trust is not granted by an executive title. It is earned.

item.title: Something big is happening in AI, but that’s the only thing Matt Shumer got right
item.description: This could be the greatest democratizing event of all time.

item.title: First, Colbert, now ‘The View’: Trump’s FCC opens equal time investigation into political chat show
item.description: “The FCC has an enforcement action underway on that,” Chairman Brendan Carr told reporters after an agency meeting Wednesday.

item.title: 5 ways to build a brand consumers find irresistible
item.description: This year has been volatile for brands. With tariffs taking effect, the job market slowing, and consumer spending barely keeping pace with inflation, ...

item.title: This math graduate applied to over 1,000 jobs and still didn’t hear back—so he moved to Austria
item.description: Gen Z and millennial workers in Britain are so fed up with being ghosted by employers that some are giving up on the country altogether.

item.title: Ikea’s Big New Announcement Says a Lot About the National Mood
item.description: Ikea isn’t the only business betting on ‘retro-innovation’ in products that bridge old and new.

item.title: How Modi’s AI handholding moment backfired as Sam and Dario refused to play along
item.description: “I didn't know what was happening,” Altman later said, adding he was “confused, like when (Modi) grabbed my hand and put it up."

item.title: Meta and YouTube are now facing a legal reckoning that harkens back to cases against big tobacco
item.description: For years, social media companies have disputed allegations that they harm children’s mental health through deliberate design choices that addict kids...

item.title: Succession Planning Is Tough, But a Solution for Aging Entrepreneurs Is Hiding in Plain Sight
item.description: Not many business owners have a succession plan in place, but SBA data hints at a smart way forward.

item.title: What the ‘AI inflection point’ means for journalism
item.description: It’s hard to tell AI news from AI hype at the best of times, but the most recent surge around agents, triggered by many developers embracing Claude Co...
